children.text: Documenting the days of District past
We are currently rebuilding our site. This section in particular requires some work to add additional content and photos, to existing and new articles.  Please revisit for new content.
If you have any content you would like to share, please send to:

mainImage.alt: Black and white photo in a book
mainImage.caption: 2003-2004 squad

seo.description: Photos and stories of the District.

children.text: They (Newbury) started with the Kick Off. We started well with a few attacks but for some reason we just couldn’t score! After 15 minutes we started to play more of a team and passed the ball around. Their defence stopped from attacking sometimes but of course we were great too. Newbury had some good attacks but we were strong and stood up to them. Second half we started with the kick off and we had an attack straight away almost got there but the defence stopped it. It was Josh on the run and it was absolutely amazing! He had a good turn over somebody then ran through the next standing people and the keeper came crashing into him and unfortunately for Josh he got injured on his calf. Later on in the game Oscar had the ball on the wing and delivered a great cross into the box which Adam had a great attempt at a bicycle kick and clipped the ball but it went wide. Suddenly the opposition had a few attacks so we built a defence to block them. All in all it was great match and ended up with the score of 0-0 but we all enjoyed it.

After he game that we should have won we got to the hotel rooms and got dressed for the exciting day ahead. Once we were ready we went to luch. The menu was mild chicken curry or jacket potatoes and dessert was ice cream, fresh fruit or apple crumble. It was all really nice.

Then we got into the bus and drove to a mini golf centre. But sadly, it was closed so we went to a different area halfway across the island (editors note: about 10 minutes away!) In the end it was worth it with 18 holes and two courses. While we there we met some Barking and Dagenham players. At the end there was a cage in it. I had Slush Puppies and crisps. Finally we found out our scores. Last was Adam and first was Alex. After, we all came back and enjoyed the day. Now we were all hungry and want an enjoyable dinner.

seo.description: They (Newbury) started with the Kick Off. We started well with a few attacks but for some reason we just couldn’t score!

children.text: We woke up very early and got our tracksuits on. We had breakfast and then we went back to our rooms got our bags and set off. The game was very intense and very even. The first half ended 0-0. The second half began and in a few minutes Jersey were 1-0 up. We had plenty of chances but didn’t take them. Thomas was fouled in the area and the ref awarded a penalty, but the chance was missed. The end score was 1-0 to Jersey.
After the game we ate our lunch, which was Lasagne or Chicken. Once we finished we played in the pool for an hour and a half. The we got changed and went to a sweet shop, and then the beach.

seo.description: We woke up very early and got our tracksuits on. We had breakfast and then we went back to our rooms got our bags and set off. The game was very intense and very even.

children.text: We started at a thrilling match against St Albans where we dominated the whole game. It all started when Joe Cotton, who was bought down in the penalty box and given a penalty, put the ball calmly into the bottom right hand corner. For the rest of the half we dominated and played by far the best football we have done for a while. Unfortunately they score early on in the second half. Finally we scored a well earned goal to put us ahead again. But unfortunately they scored late on and it ended in a draw we were gutted.

For lunch there was 8 roast chicken and 2 lasagne. For desert we had cheesecake or Ice Cream or a piece of fruit. After we went to a rocky beach it was awesome everyone enjoyed climbing and Pavel (GK) was some sort of Spiderman when it came to climbing. We all went into some rock pools and then we went back for a nice meal. We had Pork steak which was nice and Ethan had a stuffed pepper.
Note from Matthew, he says he did all the work on this, Ethan wrote the stuffed pepper line!

seo.description: We started at a thrilling match against St Albans where we dominated the whole game.

node.title: Red Squad Match and Day Report

children.text: Today we faced St.Pauls under 12 who are one of Jersey’s top teams. Stanly scored a team goal midway in the second half, and after that Charlie made a brilliant save and we held on for a 1-0 win. It wasn’t just Charlie who played well throughout the game, there were other people such as Fred, Harry, Stanly, Ollie L , Oli J,Liam,Sam,Adam,James and Max. Later on we went to the beach and James chased a ball into a waterfall twice! Dinner was a four course meal that included starters,soup,main course and pudding.

seo.description: Today we faced St.Pauls under 12 who are one of Jersey’s top teams.
